zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Philip Cumton - Comyn於1160年出生在英國戴文康普頓時，他的父親Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale45歲，母親Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl38歲。他於1229年與Lady Basilla Comyn de Compton在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1242年在他的家鄉享年82歲。

en.Castle Moulin, East Lothian, Scotland: Castle, Jackson, South Dakota, United States

nameMeanings.name: Cumton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Herefordshire and Worcestershire): variant of   Compton  .

variants.name: Compton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places throughout England (but especially in the southwest) named Compton, from Old English cumb ‘short, straight valley’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Philip Cumton - Comyn was born in 1160, in Compton, Devon, England, his father, Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale, was 45 and his mother, Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, was 38. He married Lady Basilla Comyn de Compton in 1229, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1242, in his hometown, at the age of 82.

fr.Castle Moulin, East Lothian, Scotland: Castle, Jackson, Dakota du Sud, États-Unis
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Philip Cumton - Comyn est né en 1160, à Compton, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Lady Basilla Comyn de Compton en 1229, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1242, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 82 ans.

Lui sposato Lady Basilla Comyn de Compton nel 1229, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1242, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 82 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Philip Cumton - Comyn(이)가 1160년 영국 데폰 콤프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1229년 영국 데폰에서 Lady Basilla Comyn de Compton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1242년 그의 고향에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Philip Cumton - Comyn родился в 1160 г. в местности комптон, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Basilla Comyn de Compton в 1229 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1242 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 82 лет.
